description: Stratified benchmark corpus for WI-V3-DISCOVERY-D5-CORPUS-SEED (#269). Stored side: full yakcc self-shave registry (~1,773+ atoms). Query side: 50 queries across 5 categories designed to stress D1's multi-dimensional value proposition. Per-category M1..M5 numbers are the actual input for the DEC-V3-INITIATIVE-001 measurement-first gate.

query.behavior: Return the single ASCII character at a zero-based position in a string

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for ascii-char. No guarantees or error conditions mentioned. Tests whether behavior-only matching is sufficient for a simple positional read primitive.

query.behavior: Convert a single digit character to its numeric integer value

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for digit. Short, simple description with no side conditions.

query.behavior: Verify that a parse position has consumed all input with no trailing characters remaining

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for eof-check. Tests whether an end-of-input assertion atom can be found by behavior description alone.

query.behavior: Look at the character at the current parser position without consuming it

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for peek-char. The 'without consuming' phrasing is distinctive.

query.behavior: Skip past any space and tab characters at the current position in a string

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for whitespace. Both whitespace and optional-whitespace are acceptable matches since they have equivalent behavior descriptions.

query.behavior: Parse a run of decimal digit characters from a string into a numeric value

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for integer. Tests recall of a multi-digit parser atom.

query.behavior: Match and consume a comma character at the current parser position

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for comma. Short and specific.

query.behavior: Extract a substring between a start index and an end index from a string

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for string-from-position. Uses 'substring' phrasing rather than the implementation name.

query.behavior: Compute the great-circle distance between two GPS coordinates in meters

entries.rationale: Negative-space entry. No atom in the seed registry does Haversine distance calculation. Should produce a low-score non-hit, populating M5 poor band.

query.behavior: Generate a random UUID version 4 identifier string

entries.rationale: Negative-space entry. UUID generation is not in the registry. Behavior-only category control for the no-match path.

query.behavior: Parse a decimal digit character into a number
guarantees: referentially transparent — same input always produces same output
no side effects of any kind

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for digit. Mentions purity explicitly. Tests whether the guarantees dimension helps discriminate digit from other parsers.

query.behavior: Get a character at a position in a string
guarantees: pure — no mutation of input
returned string always has exactly length 1

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for ascii-char. The 'length-1' guarantee is distinctive and should differentiate this from char-code.

query.behavior: Advance a parser position index by a fixed number of steps
guarantees: monotonic — result is always >= input position
pure — no side effects

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for position-step. Monotonic advance is the key semantic guarantee for this atom.

query.behavior: Check whether a character is an ASCII digit
guarantees: total function — always returns a value, never throws
consistent — same character always produces same result

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for ascii-digit-set. Totality is the key differentiator from digit (which throws on non-digits).

query.behavior: Read a character at a position without advancing the parser state
guarantees: null at end of input rather than throwing
pure — position is unchanged after call

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for peek-char. The null-at-EOF guarantee distinguishes peek-char from ascii-char which throws on OOB.

query.behavior: Parse digit characters from a string starting at a given position
guarantees: greedy — always consumes as many digits as possible
result value is always non-negative

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for integer. Greedy consumption and non-negative result are the key guarantees.

query.behavior: Skip whitespace characters in a string
guarantees: idempotent — applying twice is equivalent to applying once
monotonic — returned position is always >= input position

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for whitespace. Idempotency is the key guarantee that distinguishes whitespace from a one-shot skipper.

query.behavior: Parse a list of integers from a string
guarantees: pure — no mutation of input string
composed from primitive parser blocks rather than a monolithic implementation

entries.rationale: Guarantees-stressed query for list-of-ints. Composition guarantee differentiates this from a hand-rolled parser.

query.behavior: Compute a fingerprint of a string
guarantees: deterministic — same input always produces same fingerprint
no side effects

entries.rationale: Negative-space guarantees-stressed entry. No cryptographic hash atom in the seed registry. Tests that guarantees alone don't pull in wrong atoms.

query.behavior: Sort a list of numbers
guarantees: pure — does not mutate the input array
stable sort — equal elements maintain their relative order

entries.rationale: Negative-space guarantees-stressed entry. Sorting is not in the seed registry. Tests that purity guarantee doesn't cause false hits.

query.behavior: Get a character from a string at a given index
errorConditions: throws RangeError if position is out of bounds
throws RangeError if the character code exceeds 127

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for ascii-char. The non-ASCII rejection is the key distinguisher from char-code (which accepts all UTF-16 codes).

query.behavior: Consume a comma character at a parser position
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if the character at position is not a comma

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for comma. SyntaxError on mismatch is the defining error for all literal-match atoms.

query.behavior: Assert that a bracket character appears at the current parser position
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if the character does not match the expected bracket
throws RangeError if position is negative

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for bracket. Two distinct error types (SyntaxError and RangeError) are mentioned.

query.behavior: Check that parsing has consumed all input
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if any characters remain after the given position

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for eof-check. SyntaxError on trailing input is the only error condition.

query.behavior: Return the numeric char code of a character in a string
errorConditions: throws RangeError if position is negative
throws RangeError if position is at or beyond the end of the string

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for char-code. Both negative and past-end RangeError conditions are mentioned.

query.behavior: Parse decimal digits from a string starting at a position
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if no digit character is found at the starting position

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for integer. SyntaxError on missing digits distinguishes it from the total ascii-digit-set atom.

query.behavior: Validate that a string contains only ASCII characters
errorConditions: throws RangeError at the position of the first character with code above 127

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for non-ascii-rejector. The error-on-first-violation pattern is the defining characteristic.

query.behavior: Handle the case of an empty list in a parser
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if the next character is not a closing bracket

entries.rationale: Error-stressed query for empty-list-content. SyntaxError on non-']' is the defining error path.

query.behavior: Parse structured data from a string
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError on malformed input
throws TypeError if input is null or undefined

entries.rationale: Negative-space error-stressed entry. General structured-data parsing is not in the registry. Tests that error conditions alone don't pull in wrong atoms.

query.behavior: Divide one number by another
errorConditions: throws RangeError if the divisor is zero

entries.rationale: Negative-space error-stressed entry. Integer division is not in the seed registry.

query.behavior: Get a character at an index in a string
guarantees: O(1) time complexity — constant time regardless of string length
O(1) space — no heap allocation
pure — side-effect-free

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for ascii-char. O(1) time/space + pure are all standard non-functional properties.

query.behavior: Convert a character to an integer digit value
guarantees: constant time O(1) — single character operation
side-effect-free — no global state modified

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for digit. Emphasizes constant time and no side effects.

query.behavior: Parse decimal digits from a string
guarantees: O(n) time where n is the number of digits consumed
O(1) space — no dynamic allocation

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for integer. O(n) time vs O(1) space is a key non-functional profile for this atom.

query.behavior: Test whether a character is a numeric digit
guarantees: thread-safe — no shared mutable state
deterministic — same character always produces same result

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for ascii-digit-set. Thread safety and determinism are the key non-functional attributes.

query.behavior: Inspect a character at the current position
guarantees: pure — calling with same arguments multiple times returns same result
O(1) time — no string traversal

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for peek-char. Referential transparency and O(1) are the defining non-functional properties.

query.behavior: Advance past whitespace characters in a string
guarantees: O(n) worst-case where n is the length of the whitespace run
deterministic — same input always produces same output position

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for whitespace. O(n) time bound is the key non-functional characteristic for a run-length skipper.

query.behavior: Advance a position index by a fixed amount
guarantees: O(1) time — no string traversal
pure — no mutation of any external state

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for position-step. Pure and O(1) are the defining non-functional properties for index arithmetic.

query.behavior: Parse a list structure from a string
guarantees: O(n) time and space proportional to input length
pure — no I/O or global state

entries.rationale: Non-functional-stressed query for list-of-ints. O(n) space is significant since it allocates the result array.

query.behavior: Encode binary data as a text string
guarantees: deterministic — same bytes always produce same encoding
output length is exactly ceil(input.length * 4 / 3)

entries.rationale: Negative-space non-functional entry. Base64 encoding is not in the registry. Tests that non-functional constraints don't cause false hits.

query.behavior: Compute the factorial of a non-negative integer
guarantees: pure — no side effects
O(n) time where n is the input value

entries.rationale: Negative-space non-functional entry. Factorial computation is not in the registry.

query.behavior: Convert a single decimal digit character to its integer value, rejecting any other input
guarantees: pure — referentially transparent
result is always in the closed integer range [0, 9]
inverse property — digit(String.fromCharCode(48 + n)) === n for n in [0,9]
errorConditions: throws RangeError if input length is not exactly 1
throws RangeError if the single character is not in '0'-'9'

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for digit. Combines behavior + guarantees (pure, range, inverse) + error conditions. The most demanding category for D1's multi-dimensional value proposition.

query.behavior: Return the ASCII character at a zero-based index in a string
guarantees: referentially transparent — no side effects
returned string always has length exactly 1
returned character has char code <= 127
errorConditions: throws RangeError if position is out of bounds
throws RangeError if the character at position has code > 127

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for ascii-char. All three guarantee IDs (pure, length-1, ascii) plus both error conditions are explicitly stated.

query.behavior: Parse a maximal sequence of ASCII decimal digit characters starting at a position in a string
guarantees: pure — no mutation of input
greedy — always consumes as many consecutive digits as possible
result value is always non-negative
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if no digit character is found at the starting position

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for integer. Greedy guarantee plus SyntaxError on missing-digit are both distinctive.

query.behavior: Read the character at the current position without advancing the parser
guarantees: pure — position is unchanged after call
returns null at end of input rather than throwing
errorConditions: throws RangeError if position is negative

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for peek-char. The null-at-EOF guarantee and RangeError on negative position together fully characterize the atom.

query.behavior: Advance past zero or more ASCII space and tab characters at the current position
guarantees: pure — no mutation
monotonic — returned position is always >= input position
idempotent — applying twice yields the same result as applying once
errorConditions: throws RangeError if initial position is negative

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for whitespace. Three distinct guarantees (pure, monotonic, idempotent) plus a RangeError condition.

query.behavior: Assert that the parser has fully consumed the input string with no remaining characters
guarantees: pure — O(1) time, no string traversal
exact — asserts position == input.length precisely
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if any characters remain after position

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for eof-check. Pure + O(1) + exact semantic + SyntaxError on trailing input.

query.behavior: Parse an optional minus sign followed by one or more decimal digits into a signed integer value
guarantees: pure — referentially transparent
greedy — consumes as many digit characters as possible after the sign
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if a '-' sign is not followed by at least one digit
throws RangeError if initial position is out of bounds

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for signed-integer. The bare-minus SyntaxError is the key differentiator from unsigned integer.

query.behavior: Scan an entire string and reject it if any character is not ASCII
guarantees: pure — no mutation of input
reports the first violation rather than collecting all violations
errorConditions: throws RangeError at the index of the first character with code above 127

entries.rationale: Multi-aspect query for non-ascii-rejector. First-violation guarantee plus error on non-ASCII together fully specify the atom.

query.behavior: Restrict a number to lie within an inclusive lower and upper bound
guarantees: pure — no side effects
result equals lo if x < lo; result equals hi if x > hi; result equals x otherwise
errorConditions: throws RangeError if lo is greater than hi

entries.rationale: Negative-space multi-aspect entry. Clamp is not in the seed registry. Tests that multi-dimensional queries don't produce false positives from partial matches.

query.behavior: Parse a single line of CSV into a list of field strings, handling quoted fields with embedded commas
guarantees: pure — no mutation of input
handles RFC 4180 quoting rules
errorConditions: throws SyntaxError if a quoted field is not properly closed
throws TypeError if input is null

entries.rationale: Negative-space multi-aspect entry. RFC 4180 CSV parsing is not in the seed registry. The complex multi-aspect query should still produce a low score against all seed atoms.

query.behavior: Parse a human-readable duration string such as '2 days' or '1h' into a number of milliseconds

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 1: ms fixture entry. The ms package's parse() behavior — convert a duration string to milliseconds. Atoms are produced by the dependency-following shave engine decomposing ms/index.js, not hand-authored. This entry is synthetic-tasks in the bootstrap corpus (no seed block); combinedScore >= 0.70 is verified by module-graph.test.ts §10 quality test (local provider + in-memory registry populated by the engine's storeBlock output).

query.behavior: Validate whether a string is a valid email address, with support for display names, UTF-8 local parts, and configurable TLD requirements

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 2: validator@13.15.35 fixture entry. isEmail behavior — validates email address strings per RFC standards with configurable options. Atoms are produced by the dependency-following shave engine decomposing validator/lib/isEmail.js from the vendored Babel-transpiled CJS fixture (Path A). synthetic-tasks with expectedAtom:null mirrors the Slice 1 ms precedent. combinedScore >= 0.70 is verified by validator-fixture.test.ts §F quality test (local provider + in-memory registry).

query.behavior: Validate whether a string is a valid URL, supporting multiple protocols, authentication, IP addresses, and configurable options for TLD and underscores

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 2: validator@13.15.35 fixture entry. isURL behavior — validates URL strings with flexible protocol and host options. Atoms produced by shave engine from validator/lib/isURL.js. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in validator-fixture.test.ts §F.

query.behavior: Validate whether a string is a valid UUID (universally unique identifier) in versions 1 through 5, nil UUID, or max UUID format

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 2: validator@13.15.35 fixture entry. isUUID behavior — validates UUID strings across all versions including nil and max. Atoms produced by shave engine from validator/lib/isUUID.js. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in validator-fixture.test.ts §F.

query.behavior: Validate whether a string contains only alphanumeric characters, with optional locale support for language-specific character sets

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 2: validator@13.15.35 fixture entry. isAlphanumeric behavior — validates that a string is alphanumeric with locale-aware character set support. Atoms produced by shave engine from validator/lib/isAlphanumeric.js. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in validator-fixture.test.ts §F.

query.behavior: Check whether a semantic version string satisfies a given semver range expression, returning true or false

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 3: semver@7.8.0 fixture entry. satisfies behavior -- tests whether a version string matches a version range. Atoms produced by shave engine from semver/functions/satisfies.js (largest subgraph ~18 modules, exercises classes/range <-> classes/comparator circular import via Slice 1 cycle guard). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in semver-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Coerce a loose version string or number into a valid semver version object, extracting major, minor, and patch components

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 3: semver@7.8.0 fixture entry. coerce behavior -- converts loose version strings to normalized SemVer instances. Atoms produced by shave engine from semver/functions/coerce.js (~8 modules). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in semver-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Compare two semantic version strings and return -1, 0, or 1 indicating their relative ordering

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 3: semver@7.8.0 fixture entry. compare behavior -- performs numeric version comparison returning a sort-compatible integer. Atoms produced by shave engine from semver/functions/compare.js (~7 modules). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in semver-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Parse a semantic version string into its component parts: major, minor, patch, prerelease, and build metadata

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 3: semver@7.8.0 fixture entry. parse behavior (resolves issue-body 'parse-component' per DEC-WI510-S3-PARSE-COMPONENT-BINDING-001) -- converts a version string into a SemVer object with structured fields. Atoms produced by shave engine from semver/functions/parse.js (~7 modules). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in semver-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Generate a random UUID version 4 string in the standard 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal format

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 4: uuid@11.1.1 fixture entry. v4 behavior (resolves issue-body 'v4-generate' per DEC-WI510-S4-UUID-BINDING-NAMES-001) -- generates a cryptographically random UUID. Atoms produced by shave engine from uuid/dist/cjs/v4.js (~6 modules). DEC-WI510-S4-NODE-BUILTIN-FOREIGN-LEAF-001: crypto Node builtin emitted as ForeignLeafEntry. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.55 verified in uuid-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Validate whether a given string is a properly formatted UUID conforming to RFC 4122 syntax

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 4: uuid@11.1.1 fixture entry. validate behavior (resolves issue-body 'v4-validate' per DEC-WI510-S4-UUID-BINDING-NAMES-001) -- checks UUID format via regex. Atoms produced by shave engine from uuid/dist/cjs/validate.js (~2 modules). No external edges (no crypto). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.55 verified in uuid-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Generate a time-ordered UUID version 7 string that encodes millisecond-precision timestamp for lexicographic sortability

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 4: uuid@11.1.1 fixture entry. v7 behavior (resolves issue-body 'v7-generate' per DEC-WI510-S4-UUID-BINDING-NAMES-001) -- generates a timestamp-first UUID. Atoms produced by shave engine from uuid/dist/cjs/v7.js (~5 modules). DEC-WI510-S4-NODE-BUILTIN-FOREIGN-LEAF-001: crypto Node builtin emitted as ForeignLeafEntry. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.55 verified in uuid-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Generate a 21-character URL-friendly cryptographically secure random unique identifier

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 4: nanoid@3.3.12 fixture entry. nanoid primary export behavior (DEC-WI510-S4-NANOID-PRIMARY-EXPORT-001) -- generates a compact, URL-safe ID. Atoms produced by shave engine from nanoid/index.cjs (~2 modules). DEC-WI510-S4-NODE-BUILTIN-FOREIGN-LEAF-001: crypto Node builtin emitted as ForeignLeafEntry. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.55 verified in nanoid-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Parse an ISO-8601 date-time string into a JavaScript Date object including the optional fractional seconds and timezone offset

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 5: date-fns@4.1.0 fixture entry. parseISO headline binding -- parses an ISO-8601 string into a Date. Atoms produced by shave engine from date-fns/parseISO.cjs (~4 modules: parseISO, toDate, constructFrom, constants). stubCount=0 (no external edges). DEC-WI510-S5-PER-ENTRY-SHAVE-001, DEC-WI510-S5-VERSION-PIN-001. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in date-fns-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Format a JavaScript Date object as an ISO-8601 string with optional date-only or time-only representation

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 5: date-fns@4.1.0 fixture entry. formatISO headline binding -- formats a Date as an ISO-8601 string. Atoms produced by shave engine from date-fns/formatISO.cjs (~5 modules: formatISO, _lib/addLeadingZeros, toDate, constructFrom, constants). stubCount=0. First WI-510 fixture to exercise subdirectory traversal (_lib/). DEC-WI510-S5-SUBDIRECTORY-TRAVERSAL-001. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in date-fns-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Return a new Date that is the given number of days after the input Date preserving the time-of-day components

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 5: date-fns@4.1.0 fixture entry. addDays headline binding -- adds N days to a Date. Atoms produced by shave engine from date-fns/addDays.cjs (~4 modules: addDays, toDate, constructFrom, constants). stubCount=0. DEC-WI510-S5-PER-ENTRY-SHAVE-001, DEC-WI510-S5-RETURN-TO-ZERO-EXTERNAL-001. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in date-fns-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Compute the difference in milliseconds between two Date objects as a signed integer

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 5: date-fns@4.1.0 fixture entry. differenceInMilliseconds headline binding (issue-body name 'differenceInMs' resolves to differenceInMilliseconds per DEC-WI510-S5-DIFFERENCE-IN-MS-BINDING-001). Atoms produced by shave engine from date-fns/differenceInMilliseconds.cjs (~4 modules). stubCount=0. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in date-fns-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Parse an ISO-8601 date string with optional timezone offset suffix as produced by JSON.stringify(new Date()) into a Date object

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 5: date-fns@4.1.0 fixture entry. parseJSON headline binding (substitute for issue-body 'parse-tz-offset' per DEC-WI510-S5-PARSE-TZ-OFFSET-RESOLUTION-001 -- date-fns@4 ships no top-level parseTzOffset entry). Atoms produced by shave engine from date-fns/parseJSON.cjs (~4 modules). stubCount=0. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 verified in date-fns-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Verify a JSON Web Token signature using the HS256 HMAC-SHA256 algorithm and return the decoded payload

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 6: jsonwebtoken@9.0.2 fixture entry. HS256-verify behavior (DEC-WI510-S6-JWT-HS256-VERIFY-BINDING-001) -- verify(token, secret, {algorithms:[HS256]}) dispatches on options.algorithms; no separate HS256-only file. Atoms produced by shave engine from jsonwebtoken/verify.js (~3-12 modules). DEC-WI510-S6-NPM-EXTERNAL-FAN-OUT-001: first WI-510 fixture with multi-element npm externalSpecifiers (jws, crypto, ms, semver). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 gated in jsonwebtoken-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Decode the three base64url-encoded sections of a JSON Web Token into header, payload, and signature parts

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 6: jsonwebtoken@9.0.2 fixture entry. decode-base64url behavior (DEC-WI510-S6-JWT-DECODE-BASE64URL-BINDING-001) -- decode(token) returns {header,payload,signature}; base64url decoding is encapsulated by jws (external dep). Atoms produced by shave engine from jsonwebtoken/decode.js (1 module). Same atom as cat1-jsonwebtoken-parse-jose-header-001 (DEC-WI510-S6-JWT-PARSE-JOSE-HEADER-BINDING-001). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 gated in jsonwebtoken-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Parse the JOSE header of a JSON Web Token to extract the alg and kid fields for key selection

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 6: jsonwebtoken@9.0.2 fixture entry. parse-jose-header behavior (DEC-WI510-S6-JWT-PARSE-JOSE-HEADER-BINDING-001) -- decode(token, {complete:true}).header exposes the parsed JOSE header. Same source file as decode-base64url (decode.js); two issue-body headlines collapse to one atom. Same atom as cat1-jsonwebtoken-decode-base64url-001. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 gated in jsonwebtoken-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Compute a bcrypt password hash with a configurable cost factor producing a salted one-way hash for credential storage

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 6: bcryptjs@2.4.3 fixture entry. hash behavior (DEC-WI510-S6-BCRYPT-USE-BCRYPTJS-001 substitution; DEC-WI510-S6-BCRYPTJS-SINGLE-MODULE-PACKAGE-001) -- bcrypt.hashSync(s, salt) / bcrypt.hash(s, salt, cb) in dist/bcrypt.js UMD IIFE. First WI-510 single-module-package fixture. Same atom as cat1-bcryptjs-verify-001; both corpus rows point at the same merkle root. synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 gated in bcryptjs-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Compare a plaintext password against a stored bcrypt hash using constant-time comparison to verify authentication

entries.rationale: WI-510 Slice 6: bcryptjs@2.4.3 fixture entry. verify (constant-time compare) behavior (DEC-WI510-S6-BCRYPTJS-SINGLE-MODULE-PACKAGE-001) -- bcrypt.compareSync(s, hash) / bcrypt.compare(s, hash, cb) in dist/bcrypt.js UMD IIFE. Same source file and atom as cat1-bcryptjs-hash-001 (both co-housed in the bcrypt namespace inside dist/bcrypt.js). synthetic-tasks, expectedAtom:null, combinedScore >= 0.70 gated in bcryptjs-headline-bindings.test.ts section F.

query.behavior: Recursively deep-clone a JavaScript value including nested objects, arrays, dates, maps, sets, and symbols, returning a new value with no shared references

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/cloneDeep (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved cloneDeep atom can be retrieved by a rich behavioral description of recursive deep-cloning.

query.behavior: Create a debounced version of a function that delays execution until after a specified wait period of inactivity has elapsed since the last call

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/debounce (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved debounce atom can be retrieved by a description of delay-until-inactivity semantics.

query.behavior: Create a throttled version of a function that limits invocation to at most once per specified time window

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/throttle (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved throttle atom can be retrieved by a rate-limiting behavioral description.

query.behavior: Safely read a nested property value from an object using a dotted-string or array path with a default fallback if the path resolves to undefined

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/get (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved get atom can be retrieved by a description of safe path-based property access with fallback.

query.behavior: Set a nested property value on an object at a dotted-string or array path, creating intermediate objects or arrays as needed

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/set (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved set atom can be retrieved by a description of deep path assignment with auto-creation of intermediates.

query.behavior: Recursively merge own and inherited enumerable properties of source objects into a destination object, replacing arrays and plain objects deeply

entries.rationale: Behavior-only query for lodash/merge (WI-510 Slice 7). Tests whether the shaved merge atom can be retrieved by a description of deep recursive object merging.
